lines.en_lit: The large date-bulk that they said with its pit or without its pit? Rav Ashi asked a bone a barley-grain-bulk with its husk or without its husk? a wet or a dry Rav Ashi did not ask that Rav Pappa it is stated Large, as large as possible, Rav Pappa did not ask that Rav Ashi Wet is called shibbolet without its shell is called ushla.

Rava said Rav Yehuda said: The volume of a large date that they said larger than an egg-bulk. The Sages have an tradition that with this mind is settled, Less than this the mind is not settled. raises an objection An incident They brought a cooked dish to Rabban Yoḥanan ben Zakkai to taste, and to Rabban Gamliel two dates and a tankard of water. said Bring them up to the sukka, And was taught in that regard: did not because that is the halakha, Rather, they wished to be stringent upon themselves
And when they gave Rabbi Tzadok food less than an egg-bulk he held it in a cloth And he ate it outside of the sukka and did not recite afterward.
